meaning: Special letters Ta Sin that open this chapter (exact meaning known only to Allah)
they believe (referring to those who don't believe in the Hereafter)
they wander blindly (referring to those who don't believe in the Hereafter)
those (pointing to people far away who are mentioned in the next ayah)
I will come to you all
or (connecting 'bring you news' with 'bring you a burning torch')
I am bringing to you all (referring to Musa speaking to his family)
O Moses! - calling out to Moses
The Mighty One (one of Allah's names, subject of this ayah)
The All-Wise (as a name/title of Allah)
and throw! (command to throw your staff)
good conduct (referring to replacing bad deeds with good ones)
Most Forgiving (as a name of Allah)
and his people (referring to Pharaoh's people)
with it (referring to the signs they denied)
and she became completely convinced of it (referring to the signs/evidence mentioned before)
and the birds (part of Solomon's forces)
they are being arranged/kept in order
when (used for future or conditional events)
he will definitely crush/break you all (addressing the ants)
so he smiled to himself pleasantly
O my Lord! (calling upon one's Lord)
I will most certainly punish him severely
I will most surely slaughter him
so he said (connects to previous ayah)
she rules them (referring to her people)
the Satan (the one who deceives them)
their deeds (referring to the people who were prostrating to the sun)
and so he strongly prevented them
you (plural) hide (what you hide is mentioned in context)
and indeed it (referring to the letter/message)
do not rise in arrogance (against me)
and come to me! (command to a group)
the noble assembly, the chiefs (addressing the queen)
give me (all of you) your formal opinion
and they made/set/placed (referring to what kings do to a town's people)
they do/make/act (referring to the kings mentioned earlier in the verse)
with a gift (that she is sending)
and so (I am) a female watcher/waiter
with what (asking about the means/way something will be done)
rather/but rather (contrasts with previous statement about giving wealth)
you all feel joy/happiness (referring to the people receiving the gift in the ayah)
so we will definitely come to them
and we will definitely make them exit
O noble assembly! (calling out to them)
he comes/arrives to me (in jussive mood)
upon him/it (referring to the throne mentioned earlier)
this (pointing to something in the ayah)
Is it like this? (asking about the throne)
and he strongly blocked her (from worshipping Allah)
what (that which she used to worship)
a deep pool (which she saw as water)
and I submitted (to Allah's will, in response to seeing the truth)
the worlds/realms (that Allah is Lord of)
why don't you (addressing the people Salih is speaking to)
we will definitely attack him at night
and we plotted (in response to their plot)
their plot (referring to the plot of those who were destroyed)
to his people (who he spoke to)
surely you all come/approach (addressing the people of Lot)
and we caused rain to fall
Is Allah...? (questioning if Allah is better)
they deviate/turn away (referring to those who associate partners with Allah)
or who?/is it not he who?
rather (contradicting the previous question about other gods)
and he makes/sets you all (as successors)
Who is it that... (introducing a rhetorical question)
he knows (what is in the heavens and earth)
and not (they do not know when they will be resurrected)
they perceive (referring to those in the heavens and earth)
they caught up with each other
is it really that we? (expressing disbelief in being resurrected)
surely ones who are brought out
except, only (in this ayah, introduces what the previous promises were)
and (all of you) look!/so (all of you) observe! (command to group)
upon them (who are being discussed in the ayah)
it followed closely (referring to the punishment they were rushing for)
and indeed (connects to what your Lord knows)
surely he knows (what their hearts conceal)
and what/and not (connects this word to previous statement)
except (what comes after this word is not included in what was mentioned before)
Israel (meaning: Servant of Allah) - name of Prophet Jacob (peace be upon him)
they/them (referring to the Children of Israel who disagree)
and indeed it (referring to the Quran)
The Mighty One (a name/attribute of Allah)
they turned away intensively (from the truth/guidance)
a guide (for the blind from their error)
that, indeed (emphasizing what comes after it)
with/by our signs (which are being rejected)
until/up to (shows a limit or endpoint)
Did you all deny/reject? (speaking to Allah's signs in this ayah)
you all were (speaking to a group)
and so he became suddenly frightened
Allah (as the doer of the action)
you consider them (the mountains) (while you see them)
He made perfect (referring to Allah in this ayah)
you (all) do/make/act (referring to what Allah is aware of)
on that day (when the good deed is done)
and so she/it was thrown down
you (all) work/do (referring to what was done in the Fire)
and so you all recognize her/it
unaware of (describing what Allah is not)
